[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 오순택, *O Sun-taek* – also spelled as **Soon-taek Oh** or **Soon-taik Oh** or **Soon-teck Oh**; June 29, 1932 – April 4, 2018[[1]](#cite_note-1)) was a [Korean–American](/wiki/Korean%E2%80%93American "Korean–American") actor. He was the voice of Fa Zhou in Disney's *[Mulan](/wiki/Mulan_(1998_film) "Mulan (1998 film)")* and the direct-to-video sequel *[Mulan II](/wiki/Mulan_II "Mulan II")* and the sadistic Colonel Yin in *[Missing in Action 2: The Beginning](/wiki/Missing_in_Action_2:_The_Beginning "Missing in Action 2: The Beginning")*. He has starred in many films, and also acted in [television series](/wiki/Television_program "Television program"), including *[Stargate SG-1](/wiki/Stargate_SG-1 "Stargate SG-1")*; *[MacGyver](/wiki/MacGyver_(1985_TV_series) "MacGyver (1985 TV series)")*; *[M\*A\*S\*H](/wiki/M*A*S*H_(TV_series) "M*A*S*H (TV series)")*; *[Charlie's Angels](/wiki/Charlie%27s_Angels "Charlie's Angels")*; *[Airwolf](/wiki/Airwolf "Airwolf")*, *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")*; *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*; *[Kung-Fu](/wiki/Kung_Fu_(1972_TV_series) "Kung Fu (1972 TV series)")*; *[Zorro](/wiki/Zorro_(1990_TV_series) "Zorro (1990 TV series)")*; *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")*; *[The Final Countdown](/wiki/The_Final_Countdown_(film) "The Final Countdown (film)")*: *[Baa Baa Black Sheep](/wiki/Baa_Baa_Black_Sheep_(TV_series) "Baa Baa Black Sheep (TV series)")*; *[Highlander: The Series](/wiki/Highlander:_The_Series "Highlander: The Series")* and *[Touched by an Angel](/wiki/Touched_by_an_Angel "Touched by an Angel")*.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Soon-Tek Oh](/w/index.php?title=Soon-Tek_Oh&redirect=no "Soon-Tek Oh"))

Korean–American actor

In this [Korean name](/wiki/Korean_name "Korean name"), the family name is  *[Oh](/wiki/Oh_(Korean_surname) "Oh (Korean surname)")*.

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Soon-tek Oh"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Soon-tek+Oh%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(April 2018)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Soon-tek Oh** ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 오순택, *O Sun-taek* – also spelled as **Soon-taek Oh** or **Soon-taik Oh** or **Soon-teck Oh**; June 29, 1932 – April 4, 2018[[1]](#cite_note-1)) was a [Korean–American](/wiki/Korean%E2%80%93American "Korean–American") actor. He was the voice of Fa Zhou in Disney's *[Mulan](/wiki/Mulan_(1998_film) "Mulan (1998 film)")* and the direct-to-video sequel *[Mulan II](/wiki/Mulan_II "Mulan II")* and the sadistic Colonel Yin in *[Missing in Action 2: The Beginning](/wiki/Missing_in_Action_2:_The_Beginning "Missing in Action 2: The Beginning")*. He has starred in many films, and also acted in [television series](/wiki/Television_program "Television program"), including *[Stargate SG-1](/wiki/Stargate_SG-1 "Stargate SG-1")*; *[MacGyver](/wiki/MacGyver_(1985_TV_series) "MacGyver (1985 TV series)")*; *[M\*A\*S\*H](/wiki/M*A*S*H_(TV_series) "M*A*S*H (TV series)")*; *[Charlie's Angels](/wiki/Charlie%27s_Angels "Charlie's Angels")*; *[Airwolf](/wiki/Airwolf "Airwolf")*, *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")*; *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*; *[Kung-Fu](/wiki/Kung_Fu_(1972_TV_series) "Kung Fu (1972 TV series)")*; *[Zorro](/wiki/Zorro_(1990_TV_series) "Zorro (1990 TV series)")*; *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")*; *[The Final Countdown](/wiki/The_Final_Countdown_(film) "The Final Countdown (film)")*: *[Baa Baa Black Sheep](/wiki/Baa_Baa_Black_Sheep_(TV_series) "Baa Baa Black Sheep (TV series)")*; *[Highlander: The Series](/wiki/Highlander:_The_Series "Highlander: The Series")* and *[Touched by an Angel](/wiki/Touched_by_an_Angel "Touched by an Angel")*.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Oh was born on June 29, 1932, in [Mokpo](/wiki/Mokpo "Mokpo") during the period when [Korea was under Japanese rule](/wiki/Korea_under_Japanese_rule "Korea under Japanese rule"). He attended high school at [Gwangju](/wiki/Gwangju "Gwangju"), [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea"), and attended [Yonsei University](/wiki/Yonsei_University "Yonsei University") in [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul").

After the end of Japanese rule in August 1945 and before the outbreak of the [Korean War](/wiki/Korean_War "Korean War") in June 1950, Oh and his family emigrated to the United States where he attended the [University of Southern California](/wiki/University_of_Southern_California "University of Southern California"). He later earned an [MFA](/wiki/Master_of_Fine_Arts "Master of Fine Arts") from [UCLA](/wiki/UCLA "UCLA").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Acting career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Acting career")]

On Broadway, Oh appeared in the original cast of the [Stephen Sondheim](/wiki/Stephen_Sondheim "Stephen Sondheim") musical *[Pacific Overtures](/wiki/Pacific_Overtures "Pacific Overtures")*.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was an early member of [East West Players](/wiki/East_West_Players "East West Players"), an [Asian American theatre](/wiki/Asian_American_theatre "Asian American theatre") group founded in 1965.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 1995, Oh founded the [Korean American](/wiki/Korean_American "Korean American") theatre group, Society of Heritage Performers, which later evolved into the present [Lodestone Theatre Ensemble](/wiki/Lodestone_Theatre_Ensemble "Lodestone Theatre Ensemble"). From 2005, he was a professor at [Seoul Institute of the Arts](/wiki/Seoul_Institute_of_the_Arts "Seoul Institute of the Arts").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Death")]

Oh died in Los Angeles on April 4, 2018, at age 85 after a lengthy battle with [Alzheimer's disease](/wiki/Alzheimer%27s_disease "Alzheimer's disease"), according to actor Chil Kong.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Partial filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Partial filmography")]

### Films

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Films")]

* 1966 *[Murderers' Row](/wiki/Murderers%27_Row_(film) "Murderers' Row (film)")* as Tempura, Japanese Secret Agent (uncredited)
* 1967 *[Yongary, Monster From The Deep](/wiki/Yongary,_Monster_From_The_Deep "Yongary, Monster From The Deep")* as Chinese Agent (uncredited)
* 1971 *[Earth II](/wiki/Earth_II "Earth II")* as Chinese Diplomat (uncredited)
* 1971 *[One More Train to Rob](/wiki/One_More_Train_to_Rob "One More Train to Rob")* as Yung
* 1974 *[The Man with the Golden Gun](/wiki/The_Man_with_the_Golden_Gun_(film) "The Man with the Golden Gun (film)")* as Lieutenant Hip (as Soon-taik Oh)
* 1978 *[Good Guys Wear Black](/wiki/Good_Guys_Wear_Black "Good Guys Wear Black")* as Major Mhin Van Thieu, The Black Tigers
* 1980 *[The Final Countdown](/wiki/The_Final_Countdown_(film) "The Final Countdown (film)")* as Simura, Imperial Japanese Navy Air Service Pilot
* 1982 *[The Letter](/wiki/The_Letter_(1982_film) "The Letter (1982 film)")* as Ong
* 1985 *[Bialy smok](/wiki/Bialy_smok "Bialy smok")* as Tai Ching
* 1985 *[Missing in Action 2](/wiki/Missing_in_Action_2 "Missing in Action 2")* as Colonel Yin
* 1985 *[Yuki Shimoda](/wiki/Yuki_Shimoda "Yuki Shimoda")* as Himself (interviewed)
* 1987 *[Steele Justice](/wiki/Steele_Justice "Steele Justice")* as General Bon-soong Kwan
* 1987 *Biały smok* as Tai-ching
* 1987 *[Death Wish 4: The Crackdown](/wiki/Death_Wish_4:_The_Crackdown "Death Wish 4: The Crackdown")* as Detective Phil Nozaki
* 1989 *[Soursweet](/wiki/Soursweet "Soursweet")* as "Red" Cudgel
* 1989 *[Collision Course](/wiki/Collision_Course_(1989_film) "Collision Course (1989 film)")* as Chief Inspector Kitao
* 1993 *[A Home of Our Own](/wiki/A_Home_of_Our_Own "A Home of Our Own")* as Mr. Munimura
* 1994 *Red Sun Rising* as Yamata
* 1994 *[S.F.W.](/wiki/S.F.W. "S.F.W.")* as Milt Morris
* 1996 *[Street Corner Justice](/wiki/Street_Corner_Justice "Street Corner Justice")* as Kwong-chuck Lee
* 1997 *[Beverly Hills Ninja](/wiki/Beverly_Hills_Ninja "Beverly Hills Ninja")* as Sensei
* 1998 *[Yellow](/wiki/Yellow_(1998_film) "Yellow (1998 film)")* as Woon Lee
* 1998 *[Mulan](/wiki/Mulan_(1998_film) "Mulan (1998 film)")* as Fa Zhou (voice)
* 2000 *[The President's Man](/wiki/The_President%27s_Man "The President's Man")* as General Vinh Tran
* 2001 *Roads and Bridges* as Father (voice)
* 2001 *Forgotten Valor* as Colonel
* 2001 *[True Blue](/wiki/True_Blue_(2001_film) "True Blue (2001 film)")* as "Tiger"
* 2002 *The Visit* as Sujong's Father
* 2002 *[SWAT](/wiki/S.W.A.T._(2003_film) "S.W.A.T. (2003 film)")* as Sayonara
* 2004 *[Mulan II](/wiki/Mulan_II "Mulan II")* as Fa Zhou (voice)
* 2005 *Last Mountain* as Karus
* 2006 *Gang-jeok* as Jong-chae (final film role)

### Television (partial list)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Television (partial list)")]

* 1967 *[The Invaders](/wiki/The_Invaders "The Invaders")* as Houseboy
* 1968–1979 *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (8 episodes) as Robert Kwon / David Chung / Chaing / Tom Wong / Vic Tanaka / Lao / Lewis Shen / Wo Fat's Lab Technician
* 1974 *[Kung Fu](/wiki/Kung_Fu_(1972_TV_series) "Kung Fu (1972 TV series)")* as Chen Yi
* 1975–1982 *[M\*A\*S\*H](/wiki/M*A*S*H_(TV_series) "M*A*S*H (TV series)")* (5 episodes) as Joon – Sung / Ralph / Dr. Syn Paik / Korean Soldier / Mr. Kwang
* 1977 *[Logan's Run](/wiki/Logan%27s_Run_(TV_series) "Logan's Run (TV series)")* as Dexter Kim
* 1977 *[Baa Baa Black Sheep](/wiki/Baa_Baa_Black_Sheep_(TV_series) "Baa Baa Black Sheep (TV series)")* as Lieutenant Miragochi / Colonel Tokura
* 1979 *[How the West Was Won](/wiki/How_the_West_Was_Won_(TV_series) "How the West Was Won (TV series)")* as Kee
* 1980 *[Diff'rent Strokes](/wiki/Diff%27rent_Strokes "Diff'rent Strokes")* as Mr. Kim
* 1981–1986 *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")* (4 episodes) as General Nguyen Hue / Sato / Dr. Bill Su & Dr. Ling
* 1981 *[East of Eden](/wiki/East_of_Eden_(miniseries) "East of Eden (miniseries)")* as Lee
* 1982 *[Quincy, M.E.](/wiki/Quincy,_M.E. "Quincy, M.E.")* as Captain Bob Nishimura
* 1983 *[Hart to Hart](/wiki/Hart_to_Hart "Hart to Hart")* as Lang Chen-cheng
* 1983 *[Girls of the White Orchid](/wiki/Girls_of_the_White_Orchid "Girls of the White Orchid")* as Hatanaka
* 1983 *[Marco Polo](/wiki/Marco_Polo_(TV_miniseries) "Marco Polo (TV miniseries)")* as Wang Zhu
* 1984 *The Master* as Lika
* 1984–1987 *[Airwolf](/wiki/Airwolf "Airwolf")* (3 episodes) as Minh / Tommy Liu / Hiyashi
* 1985–1986 *[T.J. Hooker](/wiki/T.J._Hooker "T.J. Hooker")* (2 episodes) as Ginsu Nabutsu / Nguyen Chi
* 1987 *[Sky Commanders](/wiki/Sky_Commanders "Sky Commanders")* as Kodiak (voice)
* 1988 *[MacGyver](/wiki/MacGyver_(1985_TV_series) "MacGyver (1985 TV series)")* as Raymond Ling
* 1989 *[Hunter](/wiki/Hunter_(1984_American_TV_series) "Hunter (1984 American TV series)")* (1 episode) as Nyuen Tran
* 1992 *[Highlander: The Series](/wiki/Highlander:_The_Series "Highlander: The Series")* as Kiem Sun
* 1993 *[The Legend of Prince Valiant](/wiki/The_Legend_of_Prince_Valiant "The Legend of Prince Valiant")* as Sing Lu (voice)
* 1994 *[Babylon 5](/wiki/Babylon_5 "Babylon 5")* as The Muta-Do
* 1994–1995 *[Kung Fu: The Legend Continues](/wiki/Kung_Fu:_The_Legend_Continues "Kung Fu: The Legend Continues")* (3 episodes) as Bon-bon Hai
* 1996 *[The Real Adventures of Jonny Quest](/wiki/The_Real_Adventures_of_Jonny_Quest "The Real Adventures of Jonny Quest")* as General Yala (voice)
* 1997 *[Life with Louie](/wiki/Life_with_Louie "Life with Louie")* as Buddhist Monk (voice)
* 1997 *[Stargate SG-1](/wiki/Stargate_SG-1 "Stargate SG-1")* as Moughal
* 2000 *[King of the Hill](/wiki/King_of_the_Hill "King of the Hill")* as Monk (voice)

## Accolades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Soon-tek_Oh&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Accolades")]

In 2008, Soon-tek Oh was awarded the Lifetime Achievement Award by the [San Diego Asian Film Festival](/wiki/San_Diego_Asian_Film_Festival "San Diego Asian Film Festival").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
